topic: In China employee termination: troubleshooting guide with legal-context-first diagnostics for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination troubleshooting foreign employers
related_keywords: China Labor Contract Law termination risk
foreign employer labor dispute prevention China

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core legal framework for diagnosing route-selection and consequence errors.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports practical troubleshooting diagnosis on implementation-level gaps.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Adds dispute-facing diagnostic lens for evidence and consistency failures.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors troubleshooting for termination-month contribution and statutory closure issues.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides practical failure patterns and foreign-employer implementation cautions.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from checklist-led structure to troubleshooting-led diagnostics while keeping legal source hierarchy stable.
Excluded lower-fit generalized comparative/tax-focused commentary for this error-diagnosis format.
Retained China Briefing as practical context layer for non-local operators.
consensus: Most termination failures are misdiagnosed as process-speed issues when they are actually legal-route or evidence-coherence issues.
Troubleshooting is most effective when diagnostics start with legal context and confidence boundaries.
Foreign teams need explicit fix-paths for cross-function inconsistency before release.
uncertainties: City-level dispute handling style and evidence weighting can vary.
Complex payment-component treatment in edge cases may require local confirmation.
Official English texts may not fully capture all current Chinese-language implementation nuance.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for troubleshooting format because sources support both legal diagnosis and practical remediation sequencing.
